reasoning: The probability heavily depends on the likelihood of Bitcoin dropping about 10% (from current levels of ~$111,000) to touch the $100k mark within the ~66 days remaining until the end of 2025. The historical volatility of Bitcoin easily supports swings of this magnitude; a typical annualized volatility of 50-60% implies roughly a standard deviation of 21% over a two-month period. Mathematically, the probability of touching a barrier slightly less than 0.5 standard deviations away is over 60%. The Polymarket consensus on Oct 16 placed this at 57%. Post-Oct 16, Bitcoin briefly dropped toward $105k before bouncing back to roughly $111k. Given the recent wild swings (down from $126k earlier in the month) and the significant time remaining, the fundamental chance remains elevated despite the short-term mean reversion away from $105k. Therefore, a 62% estimate closely reflects the mathematical barrier-hit rate and recent trading patterns.
